mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is elevated and not declining, discount-rate pressure dominates near-term earnings news; higher FEDFUNDS levels reduce equity valuation multiples and predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS was 5.26 as of the latest available monthly snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if higher FEDFUNDS levels do not produce lower subsequent 20-day S&P 500 returns in walk-forward tests, or if Rank IC is above -0.03 in absolute failure direction over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A downward crossing in housing starts signals a credit-sensitive demand contraction; when HOUST falls below a key activity threshold, expected construction, durable goods, and bank-credit earnings weaken, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST was 1467.0 in the latest available snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations after HOUST crosses below 1500 have positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or no negative mean return relative to untriggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Tight policy amplifies housing weakness: when FEDFUNDS is high and HOUST is falling, refinancing and credit channels contract simultaneously, raising recession risk and predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 5.0 and HOUST is below its 6-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS was 5.26 and HOUST was 1467.0 in the latest available snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if the joint FEDFUNDS-HOUST trigger has no incremental negative predictive power versus either variable alone, or if triggered 20-day returns are not below unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in unemployment from a low base marks labor-market turning points; when UNRATE crosses upward by a material amount, earnings expectations adjust down and risk premia rise, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE was 4.6 in the latest available monthly snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day S&P 500 returns following the UNRATE trigger are not lower than baseline returns, or if the trigger's hit rate for negative returns is below 50% in rolling tests.

mechanism_narrative: Real consumption strength supports earnings: when retail sales growth exceeds consumer-price growth, nominal revenue gains are not fully inflationary and translate into positive cash-flow expectations, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: 3-month growth in RSAFS exceeds 3-month growth in CPIAUCSL
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS was 376051.0 and CPIAUCSL was 208.028 in the latest available snapshot
regime_hint: consumer-led expansion with contained inflation pass-through
failure_condition: Reject if the RSAFS-minus-CPIAUCSL growth signal does not produce positive average 20-day returns, or if its Rank IC is below 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production momentum captures cyclical operating leverage; when INDPRO is rising over recent months, manufacturing output and utilization support earnings revisions, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO was 113.8604 in the latest available monthly snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO momentum is followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in less than 50% of observations, or if average returns are not above the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation persistence during tight policy prevents a valuation-supportive easing response; when CPIAUCSL is accelerating while FEDFUNDS remains high, markets price higher real-rate uncertainty and produce negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: 3-month CPIAUCSL growth is above its 12-month median and FEDFUNDS > 5.0
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL was 208.028 and FEDFUNDS was 5.26 in the latest available snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if the joint inflation-and-tight-policy trigger fails to lower 5-day returns versus baseline, or if either standalone variable fully explains the signal with no interaction term benefit.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll momentum at low unemployment supports household income and near-term revenue expectations; when PAYEMS continues to rise while UNRATE stays below 5%, labor income growth predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS is above its 3-month moving average and UNRATE < 5.0
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS was 138122.0 and UNRATE was 4.6 in the latest available snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not have higher 20-day S&P 500 returns than untriggered observations, or if the signal turns negative after accounting for UNRATE level.
